name: HR SaaS – Swiss SMBs
product_context: We sell HRly, a cloud HR platform, to Swiss SMBs with 20–200 employees in professional services and light manufacturing. Their main pain point is manual HR admin and staying compliant with Swiss labour law (Obligationenrecht, AHV). A strong buying signal is that they are actively hiring an HR manager or scaling headcount. The decision maker is typically the CEO or head of HR. Skip companies with fewer than 10 staff, large enterprises with a dedicated HR team already on an SAP/Workday contract, or government-sector organisations.

segments.name: ProfServices
segments.description: Swiss professional-services SMBs (accounting, consulting, engineering, legal) with 20–200 staff
segments.icp_context: Swiss professional services firm, 20–200 employees, using manual HR or legacy payroll software

extra_queries: Schweiz Treuhand KMU HR Software Stellenangebote
Switzerland SMB consulting firm "HR manager" OR "Personalleitung" hiring
Zürich OR Bern OR Basel Ingenieurbüro Personalwesen Stellenangebote

segments.name: LightManufacturing

segments.icp_context: Swiss manufacturing SMB, 20–200 employees, manual payroll or legacy HR system

extra_queries: Schweiz Maschinenbau KMU 50 Mitarbeitende HR Software
Swiss medtech SMB "head of HR" OR "Personalverantwortliche" hiring
Zürich OR Winterthur OR St. Gallen Produktion Personalwesen Stellen
Swiss manufacturing company payroll software replacement

signals.name: Active Hiring
signals.description: Company is actively growing its headcount
signals.llm_definition: Mark Yes if the company has open job postings in the last 90 days, especially roles in HR, operations, or production. Mark No if there are no current openings or if the company shows signs of downsizing.

signals.name: No HR System
signals.description: No mention of a dedicated HR platform
signals.llm_definition: Mark Yes if the company website or job postings make no reference to an HR platform, HRIS, or HR software — suggesting they rely on spreadsheets or manual processes. Mark No if they explicitly mention Workday, SAP HR, Personio, or similar tools.

signals.name: Too Large
signals.description: Likely already has a dedicated HR team and enterprise system
signals.llm_definition: Mark Yes if the company has more than 500 employees, lists a Chief HR Officer, or explicitly mentions enterprise HR software. Mark No if they are clearly SMB scale.
